2604.23187.title: DyABD: The Abdominal Muscle Segmentation in Dynamic MRI Benchmark
2604.23187.paper_summary: This work introduces DyABD, a novel and complex benchmark dataset of dynamic abdominal MRIs from patients with abdominal hernias and associated high quality abdominal muscle annotations. DyABD is the first-of-its-kind in four key ways; (1) it proposes the first abdominal muscle segmentation task, (2) the dynamic MRIs are acquired whilst the patients perform various exercises, introducing extreme anatomical variability, making it one of the most challenging segmentation datasets to date, (3) it includes both pre and post corrective MRIs and (4) DyABD promotes clinical research into the high recurrence rates of abdominal hernias. Beyond dataset introduction, this work provides a comprehensive evaluation of the generalisation capabilities of existing segmentation models across Supervised, Few Shot and Zero Shot paradigms on the unseen DyABD dataset. This work reveals that there is still room for substantial improvement in the field of medical image segmentation, with the majority of techniques achieving a Dice Coefficient of 0.82. This work therefore sheds light on the true progress of the field and redefines the benchmark for progress in medical image segmentation.
2604.23187.paper_summary_zh: 這項工作介紹了DyABD，一個新穎且複雜的基準數據集，包含來自腹部疝氣患者的動態腹部MRI及相關的高品質腹部肌肉標註。DyABD在四個關鍵方面是首創的；(1) 它提出了第一個腹部肌肉分割任務，(2) 動態MRI是在患者進行各種運動時獲取的，這引入了極大的解剖變異性，使其成為迄今為止最具挑戰性的分割數據集之一，(3) 它包括矯正前後的MRI，(4) DyABD促進了對腹部疝氣高復發率的臨床研究。除了數據集介紹外，這項工作還提供了對現有分割模型在未見過的DyABD數據集上，跨監督式、少量樣本和零樣本範式的泛化能力的全面評估。這項工作顯示，在醫學影像分割領域仍有相當大的改進空間，大多數技術的Dice係數達到0.82。因此，這項工作揭示了該領域的真實進展，並重新定義了醫學影像分割進展的基準。
2604.23187.author: Niamh Belton et.al.
2604.23187.authors: Niamh Belton, Victoria Joppin, Aonghus Lawlor, Catherine Masson, Thierry Bege, David Bendahan, Kathleen M. Curran
